title: Mali jihadist blockade tightens around Bamako amid coordinated attacks
summary: BBC World reports that Mali's Jama'at Nasr al-Islam wal Muslimin (JNIM) jihadists closed routes leading into Bamako and joined coordinated weekend attacks alongside separatist factions, weakening the military government and threatening the capital's supply lines.
contents:
# Mali jihadist blockade tightens around Bamako amid coordinated attacks

## Security Situation
BBC World audio bulletin on April 30 reported that JNIM (Jama'at Nasr al-Islam wal Muslimin) jihadist forces have closed major routes leading into Mali's capital Bamako, dramatically tightening the operational blockade against the military-led government. Coordinated weekend attacks involving JNIM and northern separatist factions further weakened the regime's territorial control and undermined the Russian Africa Corps support presence.

## Strategic Implications
The Bamako blockade reflects a substantial shift in JNIM operational reach, moving from rural insurgency to direct economic siege of the capital. Fuel and food prices climbed sharply within Bamako as supply convoys faced repeated ambushes. The military government faces both a deteriorating tactical position and a credibility crisis as Russian advisers struggle to coordinate counter-insurgency operations.

## Regional Risk
The Sahel coordination between al-Qaeda-aligned JNIM and northern separatists raises the prospect of a broader Sahelian front, with potential spillover into Burkina Faso, Niger and Mauritania. Western and African Union responses remain limited following the 2023-2024 expulsion of MINUSMA forces and the deterioration of relations between AES (Alliance of Sahel States) and ECOWAS.
